rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed line 10 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) as the unsafe copy of attacker-controlled argv data into buf[256], explicitly calling it a stack buffer overflow. This satisfies localization and naming of the issue.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed strncpy with truncation and explicit NUL-termination but did not reject overlong input before copying. The criterion requires rejecting oversized input prior to copying.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended replacing unbounded string handling with bounded APIs (e.g., strlcpy/snprintf) or redesigning to carry lengths, which removes raw, unbounded string handling from the data path. This matches the root-cause remediation requirement.
